pricing_plans.price: Custom pricing (reportedly around $3/employee/month for the standard platform)

enrichment.short_description: Kustomer is a CRM-based customer service platform, once owned by Meta and now independent again, built around a unified, omnichannel customer timeline.
enrichment.starting_price: Custom pricing (Enterprise seats reportedly from around $89/agent/month)
enrichment.seo_title: Kustomer Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kustomer review 2026: the CRM-based customer service platform's features, pricing, Meta acquisition and spinout history, and top alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Kustomer is a CRM-based customer service platform founded in 2015 in New York by CEO Brad Birnbaum and co-founder Jeremy Suriel, who had previously worked together at companies including Airtime and AOL and had sold an earlier startup to Salesforce. Before any acquisition, Kustomer raised roughly 174 million dollars in private venture funding from investors including Coatue, Tiger Global Management, Battery Ventures, Redpoint Ventures, and Cisco Investments, building a reputation as a modern alternative to legacy ticketing-first help desks. The company's most consequential moment came in November 2020, when Facebook announced an agreement to acquire Kustomer for approximately 1 billion dollars, a deal explicitly intended to strengthen Facebook's (soon to be renamed Meta's) push into business messaging by pairing Kustomer's CRM capabilities with WhatsApp, Instagram Direct, and Facebook Messenger; the acquisition closed in February 2022 after a regulatory review. However, as part of Meta's 2023 'Year of Efficiency' cost-cutting push, Meta spun Kustomer back out as an independent company in May 2023, with previous backers Battery Ventures, Redpoint Ventures, and boldstart providing 60 million dollars in new funding at a 250 million dollar valuation, making Kustomer once again an independently operated business rather than a Meta subsidiary. Architecturally, Kustomer is built around a CRM-first data model rather than a traditional ticket queue: instead of treating each interaction as an isolated, closed ticket, Kustomer unifies every conversation and piece of customer data, including order history, shipping status, and loyalty information, into a single ongoing customer timeline, so agents see a person's full history rather than a fragmented case log. On top of that data model, Kustomer provides omnichannel messaging support spanning chat, email, SMS/text, and voice, plus native, deep integration with the Meta-owned messaging channels WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook Messenger, a legacy of its time under Meta ownership that remains a differentiator. The platform includes configurable workflow automation and queue-based routing, letting teams define rules for how conversations get prioritized and assigned across agents and teams, and a growing set of AI capabilities under the Kustomer AI umbrella, including a Concierge and Envoy for customer-facing self-service and deflection, and an AI Agent Team Assistant (copilot) that helps human agents draft responses and find answers faster. Enterprise-oriented capabilities include SSO and SAML authentication, data masking, a HIPAA-enabled subscription add-on for regulated industries, and a webhook and custom-attribute system (with documented limits such as 500 custom attributes per Klass, 300 brands, and 20 custom permission sets) for deep customization and integration with e-commerce and other backend systems. Kustomer's pricing is not published on its website, which instead routes prospective customers to a sales conversation; third-party pricing trackers report that Kustomer's Enterprise seat tier runs roughly 89 dollars per agent per month and its Ultimate seat tier roughly 139 dollars per agent per month, both typically billed annually with an eight-seat minimum, meaning even a five-person team is generally billed for eight seats. AI functionality is generally metered separately from the base seat price: reported figures put the AI Agent for customer conversations at roughly 0.60 dollars per engaged conversation, with an alternative conversation-based billing model starting around 0.35 dollars per conversation per month for Enterprise and 0.50 dollars per conversation per month for Ultimate, while the AI copilot for human agents is reported at around 40 dollars per user per month as a separate add-on. This pricing structure, combined with the seat minimum, positions Kustomer squarely at mid-market and enterprise customer service organizations handling meaningful conversation volume, rather than at very small businesses or solo founders. Kustomer's most direct competitors are Zendesk, Intercom, and Gladly, all of which serve the broader customer service and help desk software category; Kustomer differentiates itself from Zendesk's traditional, structure-and-reliability-focused ticketing model and from Intercom's messaging-and-chat-bubble-first approach by explicitly positioning itself as CRM-first and resolution-focused, arguing that treating customers as people with a persistent history, rather than as isolated tickets or chat threads, produces more personalized and efficient support, particularly for brands managing high-volume, omnichannel, e-commerce-style customer relationships.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Kustomer is a CRM-based customer service platform founded in New York in 2015 by Brad Birnbaum and Jeremy Suriel. Rather than organizing support around isolated tickets, Kustomer unifies every customer conversation and related data, such as orders and shipping status, into a single ongoing timeline.

The company's history includes a high-profile 1 billion dollar acquisition by Facebook (Meta) announced in November 2020 and closed in February 2022, intended to strengthen Meta's business messaging push across WhatsApp, Instagram, and Messenger. In May 2023, as part of Meta's broader efficiency push, Kustomer was spun back out as an independent company with 60 million dollars in new funding from previous backers at a 250 million dollar valuation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Kustomer supports omnichannel messaging across chat, email, SMS, voice, and, reflecting its Meta history, deep native integration with WhatsApp, Instagram Direct, and Facebook Messenger. Configurable workflow automation and queue-based routing let teams control how conversations get prioritized and assigned, while Kustomer AI tools, including a customer-facing Concierge/Envoy and an agent-facing AI copilot, add automation and deflection on top of the core CRM timeline.

Enterprise-oriented capabilities include SSO and SAML, data masking, a HIPAA-enabled subscription add-on, and an extensive webhook and custom-attribute system for integrating with e-commerce platforms and other backend systems, alongside built-in reporting and analytics dashboards.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Kustomer does not publish list pricing and instead routes prospective buyers to a sales conversation. Third-party pricing trackers report Enterprise seats at roughly 89 dollars per agent per month and Ultimate seats at roughly 139 dollars per agent per month, both typically billed annually with an eight-seat minimum.

AI features are generally billed separately from base seats, with reported figures around 0.60 dollars per AI Agent conversation and around 40 dollars per user per month for the AI copilot add-on, meaning a realistic total cost includes both the seat tier and any AI usage on top of it.

features.title: Unified Customer Timeline
features.description: Combines every conversation, order, and interaction across channels into a single CRM-based customer record instead of a ticket queue.

features.title: Omnichannel Messaging
features.description: Handles chat, email, SMS/text, voice, and social channels including WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook from one inbox.

features.title: Workflow Automation and Routing
features.description: Configurable queues and rules to automatically route and prioritize conversations to the right agent or team.

features.title: Kustomer AI (Concierge, Envoy, Copilot)
features.description: AI tools for customer-facing deflection, guided self-service, and an AI Agent Team Assistant that helps human agents draft responses.

features.title: Reporting and Analytics
features.description: Built-in dashboards and reporting on volume, resolution time, CSAT, and team performance.

features.title: Security and Compliance Controls
features.description: SSO, SAML, data masking, and an optional HIPAA-enabled subscription for regulated industries.

features.title: Extensive Integrations
features.description: Outbound and inbound webhooks, custom attributes, and native connections to e-commerce, CRM, and Meta-owned messaging channels.

features.title: CRM-First Data Model
features.description: Stores structured customer data such as orders, loyalty status, and shipping info directly on the customer record rather than as separate ticket metadata.

pros: Unifies customer history across channels into one CRM-style profile instead of siloed tickets
Strong native support for Meta-owned channels like WhatsApp, Instagram, and Messenger
Flexible workflow and queue automation for complex support organizations
AI copilot and AI Agent tools built directly into the platform
Well suited for mid-size to enterprise teams with high support volume
cons: Pricing is not published and generally targets mid-market and enterprise budgets, with an 8-seat minimum reported by third parties
AI features such as AI Agent conversations and copilot seats are billed as separate add-ons on top of base seat price
Steeper learning curve and setup effort than simpler help desk tools
Less suited to very small teams or solo founders due to enterprise-oriented pricing and complexity
Ownership changes (acquired by Meta, then spun out) have created some uncertainty about long-term roadmap continuity

faqs.question: Is Kustomer still owned by Meta?
faqs.answer: No. Facebook (Meta) acquired Kustomer for about $1 billion in a deal announced in November 2020 and closed in February 2022, but Meta spun Kustomer out as an independent company again in May 2023 with $60 million in new funding from previous backers at a $250 million valuation.

faqs.question: Who founded Kustomer?
faqs.answer: Kustomer was founded in 2015 by Brad Birnbaum and Jeremy Suriel, who previously worked together and had sold a prior startup to Salesforce.

faqs.question: What makes Kustomer different from Zendesk or Intercom?
faqs.answer: Kustomer is built around a CRM-style customer timeline that unifies order, shipping, and interaction history in one profile, whereas Zendesk is more traditionally a structured ticketing help desk and Intercom is built primarily around in-app messaging.

faqs.question: How much does Kustomer cost?
faqs.answer: Kustomer does not publish list prices; third-party trackers report Enterprise seats around $89/agent/month and Ultimate seats around $139/agent/month, both billed annually with an 8-seat minimum, plus separate charges for AI features.

faqs.question: Does Kustomer support WhatsApp and Instagram?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kustomer natively supports WhatsApp, Instagram Direct, and Facebook Messenger alongside chat, email, SMS, and voice channels.

faqs.question: Is Kustomer good for small businesses?
faqs.answer: Kustomer is generally positioned for mid-size to enterprise support teams handling high conversation volume rather than very small businesses, given its seat minimums and enterprise-style pricing.

use_cases.title: High-volume omnichannel support teams
use_cases.description: Consolidate chat, email, voice, and social messaging into one queue-routed inbox tied to a unified customer profile.
use_cases.audience: Mid-size to enterprise customer service organizations

use_cases.title: E-commerce and retail customer service
use_cases.description: Surface order, shipping, and loyalty data directly alongside conversations to resolve issues faster.

use_cases.title: Messaging-first support on Meta channels
use_cases.description: Manage and automate conversations across Meta-owned messaging channels from a single CRM-based platform.
use_cases.audience: Brands with heavy WhatsApp, Instagram, and Messenger support volume

pricing_plans.price: Usage-based (reportedly around $0.60 per AI Agent conversation, plus per-seat copilot pricing)

enrichment.short_description: Kylas is a cloud-based sales CRM for small and mid-sized businesses that charges one flat monthly fee for unlimited users instead of per-seat pricing.
enrichment.seo_title: Kylas Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kylas CRM review 2026: flat-rate unlimited-user pricing, features, pros and cons, and how it compares to Zoho, HubSpot, and Freshsales.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Kylas is a cloud-based sales CRM built for small and mid-sized businesses, founded in 2020 by Vikram Kotnis and headquartered in Pune, India. The company has grown as a self-funded business rather than through venture capital, reportedly reaching around 7.6 million dollars in annual recurring revenue with a most recent disclosed valuation of roughly 22.8 million dollars, positioning it as a profitable-leaning, India-rooted alternative to venture-backed global CRM vendors. Kylas's central product and business-model differentiator is flat-rate, unlimited-user pricing: rather than charging per seat the way most CRM vendors do, Kylas charges one flat monthly fee per organization regardless of how many salespeople are added, an approach the company markets as more predictable and cost-effective for growing teams that would otherwise see their CRM bill climb every time they hire a new rep. Functionally, Kylas covers the core workflow of a sales CRM: lead, contact, and company record management; deal and pipeline management with customizable stages; task management, reminders, and notifications to keep reps on top of follow-ups; and pre-built as well as customizable reports and dashboards for pipeline and performance visibility. The platform includes native telephony integration for making and logging calls, email sync for tracking correspondence against CRM records, and SMS and WhatsApp integration, reflecting the heavy use of WhatsApp as a business communication channel in Kylas's core Indian and emerging-market customer base. A mobile app extends core CRM functionality to reps in the field, and a dedicated field sales tracking add-on lets managers monitor visit logs, location, and deal progress for outside sales teams. Workflow automation lets teams configure rules for lead assignment, follow-up reminders, and other repetitive sales tasks, while a BI analytics add-on offers deeper pipeline and performance insight beyond the standard reporting included in paid plans. Kylas's pricing follows a three-tier structure. The entry-level Embark plan is free forever, offering unlimited users and up to 10,000 records along with lead, contact, and company management, basic pipeline management, the mobile app, task management, document attachments, and pre-built reports, making it a genuinely usable starting point rather than a time-limited trial. The mid-tier Elevate plan is priced at a flat rate reported at roughly 250 dollars per month (with quarterly, half-yearly, and annual billing options and a 7-day free trial requiring no credit card), and expands record capacity to 100,000, adds 30 workflows, email and calendar sync, SMS and WhatsApp integration, telephony integration, 30 email templates, and email support. The top-tier Exceed plan moves to custom pricing and scales further with roughly 1.1 million records, 130-plus workflows, 80-plus email templates, 125-plus custom fields, advanced security features, and phone support with a dedicated account manager. Beyond the core tiers, Kylas sells granular usage add-ons priced in Indian rupees, such as additional email templates, workflows, records, custom fields, file storage, email tracking, multi-currency support, field sales tracking per user, and access to a dedicated account manager, plus one-time onboarding and training packages, letting customers fine-tune capacity without jumping to a higher plan. Kylas's target audience is small and mid-sized business sales teams, particularly those in India and other price-sensitive or high-growth markets, that have outgrown spreadsheets or basic contact lists but want to avoid the escalating per-seat costs of adding headcount to a traditional CRM. Its most commonly cited competitors and alternatives include Zoho CRM, HubSpot Sales Hub, Freshsales, Pipedrive, and Salesforce Sales Cloud; unlike these primarily per-user-priced platforms (for example, Freshsales starting around 29 dollars per user per month or Zoho around 30 dollars per user per month), Kylas differentiates itself squarely on its flat, unlimited-user pricing model, which can be more cost-effective for larger sales teams but comparatively more expensive than per-seat competitors for very small teams of just one or two users.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Kylas is a cloud-based sales CRM founded in 2020 by Vikram Kotnis and headquartered in Pune, India, built specifically for small and mid-sized business sales teams. The company has grown as a self-funded business, reportedly reaching around 7.6 million dollars in annual recurring revenue without raising outside venture capital.

Kylas's defining feature is flat-rate, unlimited-user pricing: instead of charging per seat like most CRM vendors, it charges one flat monthly fee per organization regardless of team size, aiming to give growing sales teams predictable costs as they add headcount.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Kylas covers core sales CRM functionality including lead, contact, and company management, deal and pipeline tracking, task management and reminders, and customizable reports and dashboards. Built-in telephony, email sync, and SMS/WhatsApp integration let reps communicate and log interactions without leaving the CRM, while workflow automation handles lead assignment and repetitive follow-up tasks.

A mobile app and a field sales tracking add-on extend the CRM to reps working outside the office, and a BI analytics add-on offers deeper pipeline insight for teams that outgrow the standard reporting included in paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Kylas uses a three-tier structure: Embark is free forever with unlimited users and up to 10,000 records; Elevate is a flat rate reported at around 250 dollars per month with expanded records, workflows, and communication integrations, offered with a 7-day free trial; and Exceed is custom-priced for larger teams needing advanced security, phone support, and a dedicated account manager.

On top of the core tiers, Kylas sells granular add-ons priced in Indian rupees for extra email templates, workflows, records, custom fields, storage, and field sales tracking, plus one-time onboarding packages, letting customers scale capacity incrementally rather than jumping a full plan tier.

features.title: Flat-Rate Unlimited Users
features.description: Charges one flat monthly fee per organization instead of per-seat pricing, so teams can add unlimited users on paid plans.

features.title: Lead, Contact, and Deal Management
features.description: Core CRM objects for tracking leads, contacts, companies, and sales pipelines.

features.title: Workflow Automation
features.description: Configurable workflows to automate follow-ups, lead assignment, and repetitive sales tasks.

features.title: Telephony, Email, and WhatsApp Integration
features.description: Built-in calling, email sync, and SMS/WhatsApp messaging from within the CRM.

features.title: Reports and Dashboards
features.description: Pre-built and customizable reports and dashboards for pipeline and performance visibility.

features.title: Mobile App and Field Sales Tracking
features.description: Mobile access plus an add-on for tracking field sales team activity and location.

features.title: Custom Fields and Records Scaling
features.description: Expandable record limits and custom fields that scale with add-ons as a business grows.

features.title: BI Analytics Add-on
features.description: Optional business intelligence and analytics module for deeper pipeline insight.

pros: Flat, predictable pricing with unlimited users rather than per-seat fees
Genuinely usable free (Embark) tier with 10,000 records and unlimited users
Built-in telephony, email, and WhatsApp/SMS integration without extra tooling
Self-funded, profitable-leaning company with steady product investment
Simple setup aimed specifically at SMB sales teams rather than complex enterprise configuration
cons: Flat-rate pricing from $250/month can be more expensive than per-seat competitors for very small teams
Many features, such as extra workflows, email templates, custom fields, and BI analytics, are metered as paid add-ons
Smaller ecosystem of third-party integrations and marketplace apps than Salesforce, HubSpot, or Zoho
Primarily focused on sales CRM, without the broader marketing and service hub breadth of platforms like HubSpot
Less brand recognition and fewer public case studies outside its core India and SMB market

faqs.question: Is Kylas free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kylas offers a free-forever Embark plan with unlimited users and up to 10,000 records; paid plans start with Elevate at a flat rate from around $250 per month.

faqs.question: Who founded Kylas and when?
faqs.answer: Kylas was founded in 2020 by Vikram Kotnis and is headquartered in Pune, India.

faqs.question: How is Kylas priced compared to other CRMs?
faqs.answer: Kylas uses flat-rate pricing per organization with unlimited users, unlike most competitors such as Zoho CRM, Freshsales, or Salesforce, which charge per user per month.

faqs.question: What CRM features does Kylas include?
faqs.answer: Kylas includes lead and pipeline management, workflow automation, telephony and WhatsApp/email integration, reporting and dashboards, and a mobile app, with more advanced features like BI analytics available as add-ons.

faqs.question: What are the main Kylas alternatives?
faqs.answer: Common alternatives include Zoho CRM, HubSpot Sales Hub, Freshsales, Pipedrive, and Salesforce Sales Cloud.

faqs.question: Does Kylas offer phone support?
faqs.answer: Phone support and a dedicated account manager are included in the top-tier Exceed plan; the Elevate plan includes email support.

use_cases.title: SMB sales teams outgrowing spreadsheets
use_cases.description: Move lead and pipeline tracking off spreadsheets into a structured CRM without paying per-seat fees as the team grows.

use_cases.title: Growing sales orgs needing flat-fee scaling
use_cases.description: Add unlimited sales reps under one flat monthly price instead of renegotiating per-seat contracts.

use_cases.title: Field sales operations
use_cases.description: Use the mobile app and field sales tracking add-on to log visits, calls, and deal progress from outside the office.

features: Phone support and dedicated account manager
